title: Representation and Bias in Multilingual NLP: Insights from Controlled Experiments on Conditional Language Modeling

structured.summary: This paper scales the XLM-R multilingual masked language model to 3.5B and 10.7B parameters, training on the CC100 dataset across 100 languages. The larger models achieve state-of-the-art cross-lingual understanding on benchmarks like XNLI and MLQA while also slightly outperforming monolingual RoBERTa on English GLUE, demonstrating the benefits of scaling multilingual MLMs. The authors release pretrained models and code to support further research.

strengths.evidence: Scaling XLM-R to 3.5B and 10.7B parameters with training on 167B tokens across 100 languages.
XLM-RXXL achieves new state-of-the-art results on XNLI, MLQA, and XQuAD benchmarks, and slightly outperforms RoBERTa-Large on English GLUE.

strengths.point: Balanced performance on high- and low-resource languages
strengths.evidence: Improvements shown across diverse languages including French, Vietnamese, Hindi, Arabic, German, Greek, and Russian.

strengths.point: Public release of code and models
strengths.evidence: Authors provide pretrained models and code for community use.

weaknesses.evidence: No new architectural innovations; contributions largely engineering scale rather than methodological advances.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient comparison to other large multilingual models
weaknesses.evidence: Lacks evaluation against recent large models like PaLM or BLOOM trained on similar multilingual data.
Evaluations focus on classification and QA; absence of generation, summarization, or broader low-resource language coverage.
No ablations on data volume impact, tokenizer effects, language sampling strategies, or alternative pretraining objectives.
Missing specifics on training runtime, energy consumption, downstream fine-tuning hyperparameters, and sparse details on code/model release.
Ambiguous whether attention heads, feed-forward sizes, or positional encodings vary between model sizes.

weaknesses.point: No error analysis or qualitative insights
weaknesses.evidence: Results reported only as aggregate metrics without breakdowns or linguistic analysis.

questions.question: How would increasing training data volume or diversity affect performance at this scale?
questions.motivation: Since CC100 is smaller than mC4, understanding data scaling effects is key to optimizing large multilingual models.

questions.question: What is the impact of tokenizer vocabulary size and language balancing on low-resource language performance?
questions.motivation: Tokenizer design and sampling strategies critically influence cross-lingual transfer and fairness.

questions.question: Can alternative pretraining objectives improve cross-lingual capabilities beyond MLM at this scale?
questions.motivation: Exploring objectives like span corruption or translation prediction may yield better transfer, especially in large models.

questions.question: What are the compute cost-efficiency trade-offs and potential performance saturation points for scaling?
questions.motivation: Determining optimal model size relative to data and compute budget informs practical deployment.

critique_points.point_001: Limited novelty beyond scaling. No new architectural innovations; contributions largely engineering scale rather than methodological advances
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient comparison to other large multilingual models. Lacks evaluation against recent large models like PaLM or BLOOM trained on similar multilingual data
critique_points.point_003: Restricted evaluation scope. Evaluations focus on classification and QA; absence of generation, summarization, or broader low-resource language coverage
critique_points.point_004: Methodological details lacking. No ablations on data volume impact, tokenizer effects, language sampling strategies, or alternative pretraining objectives
critique_points.point_005: Reproducibility gaps. Missing specifics on training runtime, energy consumption, downstream fine-tuning hyperparameters, and sparse details on code/model release
critique_points.point_006: Unclear architectural scaling details. Ambiguous whether attention heads, feed-forward sizes, or positional encodings vary between model sizes
critique_points.point_007: No error analysis or qualitative insights. Results reported only as aggregate metrics without breakdowns or linguistic analysis
